strengths: 15+ years of diverse software engineering experience across cloud, AI, and infrastructure
Proven AI-agent infrastructure contributions and open-source AI/ML tooling (MCP, GISCollective)
Demonstrated technical leadership through workshops, open-source projects, and cross-stack innovation
concerns: No evidence of Master's/PhD in AI-related field or formal AI academic credentials
Lack of explicit cloud platform (AWS/GCP/Azure) product strategy or customer advisory experience
Resume name mismatch (Aisha Okonkwo vs. szabobogdan profiles) raises identity/credibility red flag
response.justification: The candidate has 15+ years of hands-on engineering experience with clear AI-agent infrastructure work (MCP code generators) and open-source AI/ML tooling (GISCollective, Fluent-Asserts), satisfying the core requirement of AI builder fluency. However, there is no evidence of cloud product strategy leadership, strategic customer advising, or formal AI education (Master’s/PhD), which are preferred qualifications. The resume’s name discrepancy (Aisha Okonkwo vs. szabobogdan) introduces a critical credibility concern. While technical depth is strong, strategic and credibility gaps prevent a high score.
